mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns show no significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL growth over a 5-year rolling window, or the correlation is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate resilient consumer demand and investment, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1.4M units
failure_condition: When both RSAFS growth and HOUST thresholds are met, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, the Federal Reserve's tightening monetary policy (rising FEDFUNDS) increases borrowing costs for corporations, dampening investment and leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL is above 3% and FEDFUNDS rises, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more often than negative, or the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing economic output and productivity, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: A consistent positive trend in INDPRO does not correlate with positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling period, with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market, which translates into higher consumer confidence and spending, supporting corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth exceeds 150K in a month, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a strong overall economy, leading to higher corporate revenues and profits, which in turn drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Over any 5-year rolling window, periods of positive GDPC1 growth do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), particularly when inflation (CPIAUCSL) is moderate, signals a dovish shift by the central bank, which lowers discount rates and encourages risk-taking, boosting equity markets.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops > 0.25% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth < 2.5% YoY
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS drops significantly with moderate inflation, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 7-year rolling window.
